title: thuja orientalis var. pendula
intro: Thuja orientalis var. pendula, also known as the Eastern White Cedar or Weeping Eastern White Cedar, is a variety of evergreen tree native to Eastern Europe and Asia, characterized by its drooping branches and dark green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of thujone and other essential oils. The health benefits of Thuja orientalis var. pendula include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
